dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S4039 IS: Professional Degree Access Restoration Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Ms. Alsobrooks (for herself, Mr. Van Hollen, Mr. Kaine, Ms. Duckworth, Mr. Wyden, Mr. Kim, Ms. Cantwell, Ms. Smith, Ms. Klobuchar, Mr. Blumenthal, Mr. Markey, Mr. Merkley, and Mr. Luján) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions

form.official-title: To amend the Higher Education Act of 1965 to reverse the reductions in Federal loan availability for graduate and professional students enacted under Public Law 119–21.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Professional Degree Access Restoration Act.

section.header: Annual and aggregate loan limits for graduate and professional students

subsection.text: Section 455(a) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 (20 U.S.C. 1087e(a)) is amended—

subparagraph.text: in the paragraph heading, by striking and federal direct plus loans;

clause.text: by striking the heading for subparagraph (A), and inserting the following: In general;
in the matter preceding clause (i), by inserting before the dash at the end the following: , for any period of instruction beginning on or after July 1, 2012;
in clause (i), by striking for any period of instruction beginning on or after July 1, 2012,; and
in clause (ii), by striking for any period of instruction beginning on July 1, 2012, and ending on June 30, 2026,; and

paragraph.text: by striking paragraph (4), and redesignating paragraphs (5) through (8) as paragraphs (4) through (7), respectively.

subsection.text: Section 455(a) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 (20 U.S.C. 1087e(a)), as amended by subsection (a), is further amended—

paragraph.text: in paragraph (4), as redesignated by subsection (a)(2), by striking paragraph (8) and inserting paragraph (7) both places the term appears;
in paragraph (5), as redesignated by subsection (a)(2), by striking paragraph (8) and inserting paragraph (7); and

in paragraph (7), as redesignated by subsection (a)(2), by striking Paragraphs (3)(C), (4), (5), and (6) shall not apply, and paragraph (3)(A)(ii) shall apply as such paragraph was in effect for periods of instruction ending before June 30, 2026, and inserting (4), and (5) and inserting Paragraphs (4) and (5) shall not apply.
